ogTitle: Warners Bay vs Warrabah: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Warners Bay and Warrabah.
heading: Warners Bay vs Warrabah — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Warners Bay, New South Wales and Warrabah, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Warrabah has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Warners Bay (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Warners Bay or Warrabah safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Warners Bay has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Warrabah scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Warners Bay scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Warrabah scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Warners Bay or Warrabah?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Warrabah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
